evals.prompt:
/analyze rfq_8f3a21 BTC 7MAY26 84000 Call

(paradigm-data-discovery → paradigm_trade_tape_slim WHERE RFQ_ID='rfq_8f3a21' returned: {"DATE": "2026-05-02", "TIME": "14:22:05", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC OPTION - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "Call 7 May 26 84000", "QTY": 50, "PRICE": 0.0122, "REF_PRICE": 0.0118, "SIDE": "BUY", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 58800, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_8f3a21", "TRADE_ID": "t_1", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_1"})
evals.expected_output: A terse block trade analysis. Two plain-text lines (header + view), then a yaml code block with [Greeks], [Fair], [History], [Live] rows. Header identifies the BTC outright call at 84000 for 7MAY26, taker Buyer, 50 contracts, fill 0.0122 above mark 0.0118. View line states spot and moneyness. Bracket rows: [Greeks] delta in BTC, theta in $/d; [Fair] bps vs mark and call IV; [History] recurrence data; [Live] bid/ask.
assertions: Response handles the /analyze <rfq_id> command form — treats rfq_8f3a21 as the rfq_id and bases the analysis on the resolved Paradigm trade-tape row
Response identifies the instrument as a BTC call at the 84000 strike for the 7MAY26 expiry, taker as Buyer, quantity 50
Response reports the fill as 0.0122 BTC above the 0.0118 mark/REF_PRICE — consistent with the resolved tape row
Response includes a yaml code block containing a [Greeks] row with delta expressed in BTC and theta in $/d
Response includes a [Fair] row inside the yaml block with an IV value for the call leg
Response includes a [Live] row inside the yaml block with a bid and ask for the instrument (or states data unavailable)

evals.prompt:
/analyze rfq_77c10b risk reversal, long 90k call short 80k put, 7 May 26

(paradigm-data-discovery → paradigm_trade_tape_slim WHERE RFQ_ID='rfq_77c10b' returned: {"DATE": "2026-05-02", "TIME": "09:11:40", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC OPTION - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "RRCall 7 May 26 80000/90000", "QTY": 100, "PRICE": 0.0045, "REF_PRICE": 0.0041, "SIDE": "BUY", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 379000, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_77c10b", "TRADE_ID": "t_2", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_2"})
evals.expected_output: A terse block trade analysis. Two plain-text lines (header + view), then a yaml code block with bracket rows. Header identifies BTC Risk Reversal, taker long 90k-C / short 80k-P, fill 0.0045 above mark 0.0041. View line describes directional exposure. Bracket rows include [Greeks] with net delta, [Fair] with per-leg IVs, [Live] with leg bid/ask.
assertions: Response handles the /analyze <rfq_id> command form — treats rfq_77c10b as the rfq_id and analyzes the resolved Paradigm trade-tape row
Response correctly identifies the structure as a risk reversal with taker long 90000-C and short 80000-P
View line (plain text, second line) describes directional exposure — long upside / short downside or equivalent
Response reports the fill as 0.0045 above the 0.0041 mark/REF_PRICE per the resolved tape row
Response includes a yaml code block containing a [Greeks] row with a net delta figure in BTC
Response includes a [Fair] row inside the yaml block with IV values for both the call and put legs
Response includes a [Live] row inside the yaml block with bid/ask for at least one leg

evals.prompt:
benchmark this fill — was it good execution?
{"description": "+1 C 7 May 26 90000\n-1 P 7 May 26 80000", "action": "BUY", "quantity": 100, "price": 0.0045, "mark_price": 0.0041, "index_price": 84200, "strategy_code": "RR", "rfqType": "drfq", "venue": "DBT", "product_codes": ["DO"], "quote_currency": "BTC", "displayValues": {"markOffset": "+0.0004"}}
evals.expected_output: A terse block trade analysis focused on execution. Two plain-text lines (header + view), then a yaml code block. A full block-trade JSON is pasted directly (not an rfq_id), so the skill parses it as-is. Header states fill 0.0045 BTC, above mark (+0.0004). [Fair] row states offset vs mark in bps and per-leg IVs. [Live] row shows current bid/ask so the reader can benchmark the fill vs current screen.
assertions: Response identifies the fill price as 0.0045 BTC and states it was above the mark (0.0041 BTC), with the offset expressed as +0.0004 BTC or in bps
Response includes a yaml code block containing a [Fair] row with bps vs mark and at least one leg IV
Response includes a [Live] row inside the yaml block with current bid/ask for at least one leg
Response does not refuse to answer — a pasted block JSON is sufficient to produce the analysis without an rfq_id lookup
Response correctly identifies the structure as a risk reversal with taker long 90000-C and short 80000-P
The [Live] row benchmarks the fill against the current screen (bid/ask or mid) so the reader can judge execution quality

evals.prompt:
/analyze rfq_rr55x75 RRPut 25 Sep 26 55000/75000 + perp

(paradigm-data-discovery → paradigm_trade_tape_slim WHERE RFQ_ID='rfq_rr55x75' returned 3 rows: [{"DATE": "2026-07-15", "TIME": "06:50:01", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC OPTION - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "RRPut  25 Sep 26  55000/75000 +0.31…  Perpetual  65,000", "QTY": 100, "PRICE": 0.0177, "REF_PRICE": 0.0176, "SIDE": "SELL", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 6491801, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_rr55x75", "TRADE_ID": "t_4a", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_4", "OFFSET_BPS": 1.0}, {"DATE": "2026-07-15", "TIME": "06:50:01", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC OPTION - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "RRPut  25 Sep 26  55000/75000 +0.31…  Perpetual  65,000", "QTY": 100, "PRICE": 0.0168, "REF_PRICE": 0.0161, "SIDE": "BUY", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 6491801, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_rr55x75", "TRADE_ID": "t_4b", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_4", "OFFSET_BPS": 7.0}, {"DATE": "2026-07-15", "TIME": "06:50:01", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC PERPETUAL - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "RRPut  25 Sep 26  55000/75000 +0.31…  Perpetual  65,000", "QTY": 207000, "PRICE": 65000, "REF_PRICE": 64955.57, "SIDE": "SELL", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 2070000, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_rr55x75", "TRADE_ID": "t_4c", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_4"}])
evals.expected_output: A terse block trade analysis of a delta-hedged put risk reversal resolved from per-leg tape rows. Header: Seller, net credit Recd 0.0009 (sold leg 0.0177 minus bought leg 0.0168), offset −6 bps below mark (net fill credit 0.0009 vs net mark credit 0.0015) — NOT the per-leg +1 or +7 values, and not '+6 bps'. Perp row treated as the delta hedge, excluded from the premium netting. yaml block with [Greeks] (net incl. perp hedge), [Fair] repeating −6 bps below mark with per-leg IVs, [History], [Live].
assertions: Response nets the two option legs to a package price of 0.0009 BTC net credit (0.0177 sold − 0.0168 bought) and presents the taker as Seller / Recd
Response reports the package offset as −6 bps below mark (net credit 0.0009 received vs 0.0015 at mark) — the header and [Fair] row agree on this one number

Response does NOT present the fill as above mark or as positive edge (no '+6 bps', '+1 bps', or '+7 bps' as the package offset) — the per-leg OFFSET_BPS values (+1, +7) must not appear as the package offset
Response excludes the perpetual row from the premium/offset netting (the perp is the delta hedge), while still acknowledging the perp leg in the structure or greeks
Response includes a yaml code block with [Greeks], [Fair], [History], and [Live] rows
Response derives structure and legs from the resolved rows (25 Sep 26 55000/75000 put risk reversal + perp), not from the user's inline description

evals.prompt:
/analyze rfq_cstm2x1 custom put ratio 24 Jul

(paradigm-data-discovery → paradigm_trade_tape_slim WHERE RFQ_ID='rfq_cstm2x1' returned 2 rows: [{"DATE": "2026-07-15", "TIME": "06:48:10", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC OPTION - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "Cstm  -2.00  Put  24 Jul 26  59000       +1.00  Put  24 Jul 26  65000", "QTY": 40, "PRICE": 0.0023, "REF_PRICE": 0.0021, "SIDE": "BUY", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 1291204, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_cstm2x1", "TRADE_ID": "t_5a", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_5", "OFFSET_BPS": 2.0}, {"DATE": "2026-07-15", "TIME": "06:48:10", "AUCTION": "RFQ", "PRODUCT": "BTC OPTION - DBT", "DESCRIPTION": "Cstm  -2.00  Put  24 Jul 26  59000       +1.00  Put  24 Jul 26  65000", "QTY": 20, "PRICE": 0.0210, "REF_PRICE": 0.0212, "SIDE": "SELL", "QUOTE_CURRENCY": "BTC", "NOTIONAL_VOLUME_USD": 1291204, "RFQ_ID": "rfq_cstm2x1", "TRADE_ID": "t_5b", "BLOCK_TRADE_ID": "b_5", "OFFSET_BPS": -2.0}])
evals.expected_output: A terse block trade analysis of a −2/+1 put ratio (long 2× 59000 P per short 1× 65000 P, per the leg QTYs 40 vs 20). Package qty-weights the legs 2:1 against the base (smallest) QTY of 20: net fill = 2×0.0023 − 0.0210 = −0.0164 credit, net mark = 2×0.0021 − 0.0212 = −0.0170 → header Seller, Recd 0.0164, −6 bps below mark. Not a per-leg ±2 bps value. yaml block with the four bracket rows.
assertions: Response qty-weights the legs by their QTY ratio (40 vs 20 → 2:1) and nets the package to 0.0164 BTC credit received (2×0.0023 paid vs 0.0210 collected)
Response reports the package offset as −6 bps below mark (0.0164 received vs 0.0170 at mark), the same number in the header and the [Fair] row
Response does not use a per-leg OFFSET_BPS value (±2 bps) as the package offset and does not present the fill as above mark
Response identifies the structure from the resolved DESCRIPTION as a −2/+1 put ratio (structure qty 20: long 40× 59000 P, short 20× 65000 P from the taker's perspective per leg SIDEs)
Response includes a yaml code block with [Greeks], [Fair], [History], and [Live] rows

trigger_evals.query: get market color on this RFQ
show me the BTC funding rate

trigger_evals_validation.query: I just got filled on a Paradigm RFQ — what happened
here's the block trade JSON, break it down for me
is this a good vol trade
show me open interest on BTC options
what's the implied vol term structure
